mechanism_narrative: Increases in FEDFUNDS over the trailing three monthly observations raise the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if trailing 3-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Positive trailing growth in RSAFS signals resilient household demand and stronger near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive trailing 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative RSAFS growth at p <= 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE crossing above its prior 6-month range signals labor-market deterioration and weaker earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its trailing 6-month maximum or rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE deterioration episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is low and FEDFUNDS is rising, tight labor markets increase inflation pressure and force policy restraint, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0 while FEDFUNDS rises over the trailing 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE and rising-FEDFUNDS condition has no lower 20-day return distribution than other periods, with Mann-Whitney p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive trailing growth in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive trailing 3-month INDPRO growth has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day S&P 500 returns over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in HOUST signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 1200 or falls more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-break triggers are followed by median 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional median return.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS is already elevated, markets reprice expected policy restraint and margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS is above 2%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-and-policy episodes produce nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 forward returns with t-test p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive trailing growth in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates income growth feeding directly into consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with both positive PAYEMS and RSAFS 3-month growth do not outperform periods lacking that joint condition for 20-day returns at p <= 0.10.
